title: sedum rubens subsp. andegavense
intro: Sedum rubens subsp. andegavense, also known as Andean stonecrop, is a subspecies of succulent plant native to the mountainous regions of central and southern Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Andean stonecrop include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Andean stonecrop can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused in oil to extract its medicinal properties, and it is also used in the production of tinctures and salves for topical application.
